input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` takes two values — `Light` and `Dark` — and the framework automatically picks the right one based on the device's current theme, updating live if the user toggles it in system settings.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme` extension method

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,    // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // Dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

## Scaling up with resource dictionaries

For a real app, define colors once in `App.xaml` and reference them by key:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <!-- won't work ↑ — AppThemeBinding can't be used inline in Color.
         Use styles instead: -->

    <Style TargetType="ContentPage">
        <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
    </Style>

    <Style x:Key="PrimaryLabel" TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />
    </Style>
</Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then your page simplifies to:

```xml
<Label Text="Welcome" Style="{StaticResource PrimaryLabel}" />
```

## Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts live** | Theme changes are reflected immediately — no restart needed |
| **Detect manually** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified` |
| **Listen for changes** | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` event |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to override the system |
